Which one is an adverb?
_______, received a law degree as today.
What does the expression ‘to burn the midnight oil’ mean?
options_html: To stay awake all night working
To work hard to make money
correct_answer_html: To stay awake all night working

question_html: Make it passive: “He annoyed me.”
Change the narration: He said to me, “Let us go home together.”
options_html: He proposed to me to go home together
He proposed to me that we should go home together
He asked me to go home with him
He urged me to go home with him
correct_answer_html: He proposed to me that we should go home together

question_html: Make it affirmative: Unless you eat, you will fall sick.
Only by guessing _____.
Make it negative: “He tried all plans.”
options_html: He did not try all plans.

question_html: Make it indirect: Shifat said, “I must write a letter.”
options_html: Shifat said he must write a letter.
Shifat said that he must write a letter.
Shifat said he had to write a letter.
Shifat said that he had to write a letter.
correct_answer_html: Shifat said that he had to write a letter.

question_html: ‘Burning the candle of both ends’ means-
Which of the following sentences contains ‘The’ as an adverb?
options_html: The honesty of the girl pleased me

question_html: “My grandparents look after me.”- Make it passive.
options_html: I am looked by me grandparents after.
I am looked after by my grandparents.
I was looked after by my grandparents.
My grandparents were looked after by me.
correct_answer_html: I am looked after by my grandparents.

question_html: Turn to the direct form of speech: Rani said that her brother had been getting married.
options_html: Rani said, “Her brother is getting married.”
Rani told, “My brother has gotten married.”
Rani said, “My brother is getting married.”
Rani said, “My brother was getting married.”
correct_answer_html: Rani said, “My brother was getting married.”

question_html: Make it interrogative: “Our life is nothing but a walking shadow.”
options_html: Is our life nothing but a walking shadow?
What is our life but a walking shadow?
Is our life something but a walking shadow?
Is our life nothing without a walking shadow?
correct_answer_html: What is our life but a walking shadow?

question_html: The direct form of the indirect sentence: “He said that the patient had died early in the morning.”
options_html: He said, “The patient had been died early in the morning.”
He said, “The patient had died early in the morning.”
He said, “The patient died early in the last morning.”
He said, “The patient died early in the morning.”
correct_answer_html: He said, “The patient died early in the morning.”
